中文 GitHub

Native analytics for serious AI workflows

See every token, quota window, and session arc without leaving your menu bar.

Claude Statistics turns local AI coding history into a polished macOS command center for Claude Code, Codex CLI, and Gemini CLI.

Download latest release See screenshots
  • Local-first transcript parsing
  • Quota and cost monitoring
  • Multi-account and CLI switching
All providers Claude + Codex + Gemini

Switch providers instantly and keep each parsing pipeline running independently.

Usage windows
5h / 7d / grouped quota tracking
Local-first Analytics from transcripts you already own
3 AI coding providers in one app
53-week heatmap and all-time trends
Local parsing, search, and cost analysis
10 + 11 share roles and unlockable badges

Why it feels different

A native-feeling command center with the visual confidence of a modern product site.

Claude Statistics turns local AI coding history into a polished macOS command center for Claude Code, Codex CLI, and Gemini CLI.

3 AI coding providers in one app
53-week heatmap and all-time trends

Session intelligence

Search transcripts, inspect tools, and jump back into the exact project context.

Browse grouped sessions, inspect token and model breakdowns, and reopen work in your preferred terminal.

Usage visibility

Track live quota pressure before a workflow stalls.

Follow 5-hour and 7-day Claude windows, Gemini grouped buckets, and menu bar status at a glance.

All-time analytics

See the shape of your work over weeks, months, and bursts.

Heatmaps, project rankings, trend charts, and cost distribution turn raw transcripts into signal.

Provider-aware design

Claude, Codex, and Gemini each keep their own parsing and account model.

Provider switching feels instant without stopping background indexing for the others.

Account switching

Swap live Claude, Codex, or Gemini identities without digging through dotfiles.

Changing CLI identity becomes a product feature, not a shell ritual.

Share cards

Turn deep usage history into polished, data-backed bragging rights.

Generate role-driven cards with badges, proof metrics, and export-ready visuals.

What the app actually covers

More than a dashboard: session control, transcript search, provider switching, and operational detail.

01

Session control

Recent sessions, grouped projects, hover actions, and fast resume flows.

  • Search by path, topic, session name, or session ID
  • Resume, start new, delete, batch delete, and copy path quickly
  • Real-time file watching where provider formats allow it
02

Transcript viewer

Read conversations in-app instead of opening raw JSONL or CLI files.

  • Tool-call aware rendering with role-specific transcript display
  • Match navigation, highlight, and Markdown code block support
  • Better visibility into what tools were actually used
03

Analytics

All-time, daily, weekly, and monthly views with real cost signal.

  • Heatmap, project rankings, period drill-down, and trend charts
  • Token, model, cache-read, and cache-write breakdowns
  • Context usage and per-session model distribution
04

Quota monitoring

Watch usage windows before they surprise you mid-flow.

  • Claude 5h and 7d tracking plus provider-aware menu bar status
  • Gemini grouped quota buckets and local usage trends
  • Auto-refresh, retry states, and clearer local usage visibility

Accounts and CLI identity

Switch accounts inside the app, including the underlying CLI account it activates.

Claude Statistics can keep managed accounts for Claude, Codex, and Gemini, then switch the live account used by that provider when you choose another identity.

  • Add new provider accounts from Settings instead of manually editing auth files
  • Switch the active Claude, Codex, or Gemini CLI identity from the same panel
  • Keep provider switching and account switching separate so tool and user can change independently
  • Pair each workflow with your preferred terminal for fast session resume

Share preview

Turn heavy usage history into a polished identity card people will actually remember.

Share Preview is not a generic export. It composes role labels, badge unlocks, cost and token proof, project coverage, and a QR-ready layout into something worth posting.

10 role archetypes
11 badge unlocks
PNG native export
  • 10 role archetypes and 11 unlockable badges based on actual usage patterns
  • Native-resolution PNG export plus copy, save, and social share actions
  • Built-in QR block so a card can point back to your repo, profile, or launch post
Local-first Turn deep usage history into polished, data-backed bragging rights.

Share cards turn usage history into something visual enough for social posts and team chats.

Settings and integrations

The operational layer matters too: terminals, pricing, refresh rules, status lines, and updates.

Workflow settings

Preferred terminal, launch-at-login, language, font scale, and tab order.

Built for daily use, not one-off inspection.

Pricing and status line

Edit model pricing, fetch updates, and install provider-specific status line integrations.

Useful when you care about cost fidelity and want CLI context visible before opening the app.

Diagnostics and updates

Export diagnostics, inspect failures, and stay current with Sparkle-based updates.

A tool you can trust operationally, not just a pretty layer over transcript files.

Share system

10 roles, 11 badges, QR code embeds, and native-resolution PNG export.

Share cards turn usage history into something visual enough for social posts and team chats.

Product gallery

Everything important stays one click away.

Ready to ship

Download the app or inspect the source.

Install the latest DMG from GitHub Releases, or clone the repository and build it locally with XcodeGen and Xcode 16+.

Download latest release Browse source

Claude Statistics is a native macOS menu bar app for AI session analytics.

Built for people who use Claude Code, Codex CLI, and Gemini CLI enough to care about context, cost, and momentum.
